details: Mandate: BUILD and MEASURE NoC-mcast KV read-once variant of SDPA on Blackhole. Override prior roofline-only STOP. Target shape 1x10x9472x128 bf16 MHA self-attn non-causal.

Read perf_findings, op_design, program_descriptor, reader/compute/writer kernels, mcast_pipe.hpp, and studied shared_input_reuse reference. Mcast requires KV-outer restructure: each core holds multiple q-chunk (m,l,O) states resident; injector reads KV once per head and mcasts to group rectangle. Plan: dedicated guarded mcast reader+compute+writer, existing Q-outer path untouched.

Wrote 3 mcast kernels: mcast_compute (KV-outer, MAX_SUBCHUNK=4 resident state, unfused helpers + fast_exp), mcast_reader (IS_SENDER + mcast_bcast flags, Mcast1D one family K then V), mcast_writer (drains q_cnt output blocks). Next: descriptor guarded branch with Mcast1D PerRow wiring.

First run FAILED: TT_FATAL Program size 133968 > kernel config buffer 70656 (compute binary too big from 4x unroll). Rewrote compute as SINGLE code path: rotating state CBs (row_max/row_sum/out_accum hold q_cnt blocks, one rotation per KV chunk) + In0SourceFn to read q_scaled[s] via runtime CB index. CB count 28->21. Re-testing.

Root cause: OperandKind::Block indexes held operand ABSOLUTELY (base+i, slot 0), not front-relative -> stale read on rotating multi-block ring for q_cnt>=2. Fix: pop cb_row_max front into depth-1 cb_m_old scratch before held/Block math; normalize recips into cb_l_tmp scratch. q_cnt=1 already correct. Now PCC~0.9997 on (1,2,3072,64),(1,1,4096,64),(1,3,3072,128).

All correctness green: target (1,10,9472,128) perf test PCC>=0.997; 13 main unit tests (baseline path, no regression); 6 mcast tests incl target + golden long-context (4096/8192) under --dev (no hang/race). Target uses Skv_chunk_t=4 (74 KV chunks), subchunk=4.

MEASURED NEGATIVE (roofline confirmed). baseline Q-outer 5.284ms; mcast bcast-ON 7.626ms (+44.3%); mcast bcast-OFF(KV-outer redundant DRAM) 7.654ms; mcast compute-floor(rdr+wtr stub) 7.620ms. Regression is ENTIRELY the KV-outer restructure raising the compute floor 4.888->7.620ms (+56%). Broadcast itself neutral/slightly + (7.626 vs 7.654, saves 0.028ms of exposed reads) but reads ~99.9% hidden (floor 7.620~=e2e 7.626, exposed reads+writes+handshake only 0.006ms). Roofline cost (i) 10:1 serial dep + (ii) congestion: exposed ~0 (hidden behind dominant compute); cost (iii) restructure raises floor: CONFIRMED, the killer. Read volume 1.8GB->~48MB (37x) real but worthless. Skv_chunk_t forced to 4 (74 KV chunks) by 4 resident subchunk states not fitting L1 at 8 -> a restructure consequence.

Flipped mcast to OPT-IN (SDPA_MCAST=1), default OFF, because measured +44% regression -> must not be the default shipped path. Fast Q-outer baseline stays default for every shape (zero regression). mcast test sets SDPA_MCAST=1 via autouse fixture. perf_findings + descriptor env docs updated. Task complete.

Team lead: build Q-outer LOCKSTEP mcast FIRST (priority), not KV-outer. Keep Q-outer (ONE (m,l,O) per core -> floor stays 4.888ms), only change (1) head-grouped rectangles + (2) lockstep KV inner loop across rectangle. KEY: reuse the EXISTING optimized compute kernel UNCHANGED (fused, fast_exp). num_waves=ceil(n_q_chunks/cores_per_row)=4; clamp-pad duplicates fill idle 4th-wave slots (redundant same-value writes, no idle-receiver hang, critical path stays 4 q-chunks = baseline). New lockstep reader (injector reads+mcasts KV once per wave) + new writer (per-wave qc). Gate SDPA_LOCKSTEP=1. KV-outer stays behind SDPA_MCAST=1.

Q-outer lockstep variant CORRECT: small (1,2,3072,64)/(1,3,3072,128) PCC 0.9997, target (1,10,9472,128) PCC 0.9997, all under --dev no hang. Reuses baseline compute kernel VERBATIM + new lockstep reader/writer + head-grouped Mcast1D. SDPA_LOCKSTEP=1 opt-in. Next: measure e2e + floor.

Q-outer lockstep is a WIN. Same-session: baseline 5.275ms -> lockstep 4.921ms = -6.7% (1.072x), stable x2. Compute FLOOR 4.888ms = baseline floor EXACTLY (thesis CONFIRMED: grouping+lockstep did NOT raise floor). Lockstep reads-visible 0.033ms (0.67%) vs baseline 0.387ms (7.3%) -> broadcast cut DRAM contention (10 injectors vs 110 readers), hid nearly all reads. Lockstep bcast-OFF 8.183ms (per-wave redundant re-reads hammer DRAM -> broadcast essential). Lockstep captures ~90% of the baseline read-headroom ceiling (4.896ms). Barrier does NOT stall (e2e within 0.7% of floor -> injector read+mcast hidden behind compute, double-buffered). Read volume 1.8GB->~192MB.

Team lead redirect (detailed Q-outer lockstep spec) arrived AFTER I built+measured exactly it. All constraints match: reused shipped fused Q-outer compute VERBATIM (Skv_chunk_t=8, 37 KV chunks, one (m,l,O)), only reader+wiring changed, opt-in SDPA_LOCKSTEP=1. Thesis PASSED: floor 4.888ms preserved, e2e 4.921ms = 6.7% WIN, broadcast SHAVED exposed reads 0.387->0.033ms. Finishing validation + perf_findings subsection.

7/7 lockstep correctness tests pass under --dev (golden long-context 4096/8192, target, clamp-pad edge case), 13/13 baseline unchanged. perf_findings § NoC-multicast now has TWO measured subsections: VARIANT A Q-outer lockstep (+6.7% WIN, floor 4.888ms preserved) and VARIANT B KV-outer (+44% loss). Committing.
